name: Nicotinamide riboside reached aged human muscle but did not improve bioenergetics
summary: NR increased aged human skeletal-muscle NAD-metabolome readouts and shifted inflammatory signatures, but mitochondrial bioenergetics did not improve.
statement: In 12 aged men, 1 g/day nicotinamide riboside for 21 days increased skeletal-muscle NAD-metabolome markers and lowered circulating inflammatory cytokines, but did not improve measured mitochondrial bioenergetics.
population_or_model: Aged men, median age about 75 years
quantitative_note: The crossover trial included 12 participants and used muscle biopsy, metabolomics, transcriptomics, and mitochondrial-respiration assays.
caveats: The study was short and restricted to men.
The bioenergetic null result limits claims that NAD metabolite movement translates to better mitochondrial function.
